title: Upstream onboarding (Google Fonts / Noto) or named community-maintainer handoff
context: Per Elyos's definition of delivered, a font must be *actually adopted* to count as done. M4 ensures long-term sustainability: at least one family must be accepted into a durable upstream (Google Fonts or Noto) or have a named community maintainer committed in writing. This prevents the fonts from becoming orphaned after project completion. Depends on the Latin family (fonts-ship-104) and ideally the script family (fonts-script-204) being released first.
objective: Submit at least one shipped font family for acceptance into Google Fonts or Noto, or secure a committed named community maintainer in writing, meeting upstream specs (METADATA, license, FONTLOG, build) and documenting the handoff.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one family is submitted for acceptance into a durable upstream (Google Fonts or Noto) via a formal submission PR, or a named community maintainer commits in writing to maintain the font.
Repository meets upstream specs: METADATA, OFL.txt, FONTLOG.txt, reproducible build, and FontBakery pass.
Submission or handoff is documented in the repository; the named maintainer or upstream acceptance is recorded.
If the upstream submission is rejected, the rejection reason is documented and a named community maintainer alternative is pursued.
No secrets, tokens, or API keys are introduced in the upstreaming PR or handoff documentation.
output: A pull request to Google Fonts or Noto upstreaming at least one shipped family, or documented written commitment from a named community maintainer, with the handoff recorded in the project repository.
